rulings.Case Title: CAVALRY PORTFOLIO SERVICES, LLC VS. ALICIA F. GARCIA et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 27, 2018, Line 9, PLAINTIFF CAVALRY PORTFOLIO SERVICES, LLC'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION. The hearing on the claim of exemption is OFF CALENDAR as moot. The Sheriff informed the court that he ordered the funds released because he did not receive a timely notice of opposition to the claim of exemption. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ELEN MEDER ET AL VS. COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, HUMAN SERVICES AGENCY,
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 27, 2018, Line 1, PETITIONERS ELEN MEDER, WALTER MEDER'S Notice of Hearing On Petition for Writ of Mandate. Petitioner Meders' petition for writ of mandate is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. The respondent's opposition indicates that the administrative record has yet to be prepared and the court cannot entertain this motion without the administrative record. (See CCP 1094.5.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for respondent is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: DARYL WOODRUFF VS. PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Production, Responses And A Privilege Log, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Friday, April 27, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF DARYL WOODRUFF'S Motion To Compel Further Production, Responses And A Privilege Log, And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required to discuss each of the RFPDs at issue. In particular, the Court requests argument on the breadth of the requests and whether documents responsive to those requests have already been produced or are being withheld. Prior to the hearing, counsel are directed to engage in one last meet and confer discussion to narrow the issues. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MIGUEL MANUEL GARCIA VS. ESTEVEZ MARGARITO ET AL

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 27, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANT ESTEVEZ MARGARITO'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-17-563381, CGC-18-564535 WITH CASE CGC-17-556319. Margarito's motion to consolidate is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Cases CGC-17-563381, CGC-18-564535 and CGC-17-556319 are consolidated for all purposes. CGC-17-556319 is designated the lead case and all further filings shall be under that case number. The trial date of June 4, 2018 in case CGC-17-556319 is vacated. rulings.Case Title: BAUER INTELLIGENT TRANSPORTATION, INC. VS. ONTRACK EVENT MANAGEMENT, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Defendants Request For Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Friday, April 27, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANTS ONTRACK EVENT MANAGEMENT, INC. AND JIMMY ENGELMAN'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Defendants Request For Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted in part. It appears that a verification and signed responses have now been served, so those points are moot. However, the responses to RFPD Nos. 2-3, 5-6 are not code compliant because they list a series of documents but do not state whether all responsive documents will be produced. Supplemental responses and responsive documents to be served by May 9, 2018. Sanctions in the amount of $450 awarded to Defendants. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: CAVALRY SPV I. LLC. VS. ORZELL T LONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 27, 2018, Line 5, Defendant Orzell T. Long's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Defendant Long's motion for judgment on the pleadings on plaintiff Cavalry SPC I, LLC's complaint is DENIED. The face of the complaint does not disclose that the action is time barred. Paragraphs 9 and 17 of the complaint allege that Long defaulted on the account on 9/1/13. Under CCP 337, Cavalry had four years to commence this action from that date and timely filed this action on 5/30/17. Long fails to explain how the alleged burglary altered the running of the statute of limitations. Cavalry is not required to plead the particular details of its purchase of the Citibank account. In addition, Exhibit 1 attached to the complaint indicates that the sale occurred in 2/17. The court finds that Long's citation to Komarova v. National Credit Acceptance, Inc. (2009) 175 Cal.App.4th 324 is inapplicable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for Cavalry is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)
